reasoning: The document contains a total of 6 major defects, combining 5 structural programmatic issues and 1 LLM-identified visual issue. These include retained headers/footers across most pages, truncated alt text for 16 images, a full-page image duplication, multiple table markup errors, and reordered/orphaned images. Because the total number of major defects exceeds the maximum threshold of 5, the document must be routed to REJECT.

concerns.analysis: [Downgraded: image_count_ratio=1.06, placeholder_ratio=0.00 contradict LLM claim] In the source PDF, page 7 contains three images: two side-by-side images making up Figure 1 (left: hydrilla, right: coontail) followed by the caption for Figure 1, and then a single image for Figure 2 followed by its caption. In the HTML output, these images are reordered: the first image for Figure 1 is shown with the Figure 1 caption, followed immediately by the Figure 2 image and its caption. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 7 shows Figure 1 composed of two images side-by-side (left: hydrilla; right: coontail) positioned before Figure 2.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML places the Figure 2 image and caption before the right-side image for Figure 1, which appears as a standalone element at the bottom of that section.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 1 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=1. Examples: p6: "Roanoke Rapids". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (35): 2023 Roanoke Rapids Vegetation Survey Report; 1; 2023 Roanoke Rapids Vegetation Survey Report
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=35, pages_affected=18/19. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=0, no_th_tables=1. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 16 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (16 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...y with wispy clouds. A rocky, muddy shoreline with"; p1: "...in white uppercase letters. The words NC STATE are"; p7: "...nd brownish aquatic plants growing thickly beneath"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=16 (truncated=16, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

On page 1, the headings 'Prepared By:' and 'Submitted To:' are both italicized and underlined in the source PDF. The JSON extraction captures the italics (via markdown asterisks) but entirely drops the underline emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Prepared By: (underlined in source)
Submitted To: (underlined in source)

concerns.analysis: Matched tables have >20% row count difference (delta >=3). Pages: p6(source:10rows,output:18rows)
Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p6(source:5cols,output:4cols)

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://www.dominionenergy.com/projects-and-facilities/hydro']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.
